claim: Be specific in AI prompts; 'make the edit button toggle' is lazy; instead specify 'add boolean field editable to users table, expose via API, conditionally render button'
actionable: Write prompts that are testable and concrete, removing margin for error and hallucination through specific implementation details
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Set up global rules (in warp drive or similar) for coding standards, libraries, testing philosophy, and project-specific rules for stack and database schema
actionable: Create two rule files: global for preferences across all projects, project-specific (warp.md) for tech stack and API patterns
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Index your full codebase context first so AI has awareness of entire architecture, then create rules to avoid repetition in prompts
actionable: Use 'codebase context' feature in AI tools to index the full project before prompting so AI understands architecture without re-specifying it
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Add context to prompts by referencing specific files or highlighting code to reduce tokens and improve specificity
actionable: Reference exact file paths and highlight code snippets in prompts rather than asking AI to search through codebase
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Plan first with AI before coding; break down implementation into concrete steps, review the plan for correctness, then add restrictions when implementing
actionable: Ask AI to create implementation plans before coding; review them, specify constraints (file limits, no new deps), then execute with auto-accept
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Treat AI code review like pair programming with a junior: ask why, what are edge cases, how does this impact performance, and engage collaboratively
actionable: Review AI output actively; question implementation choices, understand the reasoning, and don't blindly accept code
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Keep conversations short and focused per task because conversation history is prepended as context, increasing costs the longer the conversation
actionable: Start new conversations for each distinct coding task to avoid accumulating history that increases token usage
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Specify which model to use rather than auto-picking; use GPT-5 for planning, Opus for coding, and avoid mid-conversation model switching to preserve context
actionable: Create agent profiles that specify planning model (GPT-5) vs coding model (Claude) to optimize cost and quality per phase
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Run multiple agents in parallel on different git worktrees for independent tasks, then review and approve each as it completes
actionable: Use git worktrees to run 2-3 independent AI agents in parallel on different features, coordinating across them like Magnus Carlsen vs 20 chess players
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: Upload screenshots to show UI bugs or missing features rather than trying to describe them in text
actionable: When you encounter a UI bug or need to implement a visual feature, screenshot it and upload to AI to provide context
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI

claim: AI won't make you a 10x developer, but will help with pain points, speed up coding, explore codebases, and write tests without dread
actionable: Use AI for leverage on tedious tasks (setup, boilerplate, exploration) while focusing your expertise on architecture and critical decisions
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Coding with AI
